Program_Info.Program_System_Requirements: Windows 10 or 11 (64-bit). Administrator rights required to access C:\Windows\Installer. The slim build needs the .NET 10 Desktop Runtime; the setup and portable builds are self-contained.

English.Keywords: installer, msi, msp, cleanup, windows installer, patch cleaner, disk space, orphaned files, system utility, free, open source
English.Char_Desc_45: Clean orphaned Windows Installer cache files.
English.Char_Desc_80: Find and safely move or delete orphaned MSI and MSP files from C:\Windows\Installer.
English.Char_Desc_250: InstallerClean scans C:\Windows\Installer for MSI and MSP files no longer registered to any installed product, then lets you move them to a folder of your choice (so you can restore them if anything breaks) or send them to the Recycle Bin. Open source, MIT licensed.
English.Char_Desc_450: InstallerClean scans C:\Windows\Installer for MSI and MSP files no longer registered to any installed product. It moves orphaned files to a folder of your choice so you can restore them if anything breaks, or sends them to the Recycle Bin. Useful on machines with heavy MSI-based software (Adobe Acrobat, AutoCAD, Office) where the cache can grow into tens of gigabytes. A modern, open source replacement for PatchCleaner.
English.Char_Desc_2000:
InstallerClean removes orphaned MSI and MSP files from C:\Windows\Installer, the hidden cache Windows uses to repair and update installed software. Files that no longer belong to any registered product can sit in the cache forever and grow into tens of gigabytes on machines with heavy MSI-based software such as Adobe Acrobat, AutoCAD or Microsoft Office.
The app scans the cache, queries the Windows Installer API to enumerate registered files, and shows you what's left over. Anything not registered is presented as a candidate to move or delete.
The default action is Move, not Delete: orphans go to a folder you choose, so you can put them back if something refuses to update or repair afterwards. Delete still routes through the Recycle Bin so files are not permanently lost.
Other safety measures include a hard block on cleaning during an active Windows Installer transaction (so an install or repair in flight cannot be corrupted), refusal to move into reparse points or back into the installer cache, and a confirmation step before every Move and Delete operation.
A separate console executable (installerclean-cli.exe) is included in the setup build for scheduled tasks and unattended use, with three-state exit codes for scripting.
The application is open source under the MIT licence. Setup, portable and slim distributions are available. The setup and portable builds are self-contained and require no .NET runtime; the slim build is framework-dependent and needs the .NET 10 Desktop Runtime.
Modern replacement for PatchCleaner, which was last updated in March 2016.

Permissions.Distribution_Permissions: InstallerClean is open source under the MIT licence. You may host the binaries on your download site, mirror them, redistribute them and bundle them in compilations, provided the MIT licence text is preserved alongside the binary. The source repository is at https://github.com/no-faff/InstallerClean.
Permissions.EULA: The MIT licence, full text at https://github.com/no-faff/InstallerClean/blob/main/LICENSE.
